entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 2 Aug, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 2 Aug, 2022 published at 2022-08-02 04:50AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Australia news LIVE: RBA hikes interest rates to 1.85 per cent; COVID experts say winter wave appears to have peaked
  • Rates calculator: How much extra will your mortgage repayments cost?
  • Led many overseas Australian diplomatic posts
  • Reserve Bank lifts rates for historic fourth consecutive month
  • From the Archives, 1918: Lavish opening of Australia House in London
  • ‘A strange, strange race’: Peter Bol’s Commonwealth Games event is a contact sport
  • ‘Building resilience’: New Zealand to fund Samoa climate projects
  • Judge blasts McGowan, Palmer for wasting court time after defamation case ends in draw
  • Mitch Catlin resigns, Labor to refer Guy and staffer to watchdogs
  • Sydney swimming coach facing fresh charges of historical sexual abuse
  • ‘It’s a free market’: Patterson backs Warner to explore T20 options
  • How to save thousands in interest on your home loan
  • Man jailed over fatal bashing in BMW robbery
  • Bid to stop $8 billion superannuation claim for bureaucrats’ rent-free homes
  • Best of cartoons, August 2, 2022
  • Wyatt finds Voice on Indigenous referendum
  • Operation COVID Shield ends as vaccination booster rates stall
  • The latest illustrations from artist Andrew Dyson
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • The latest illustrations from artist Cathy Wilcox
  • Reserve Bank poised for historic fourth consecutive hike
  • What’s an Indigenous Voice to parliament? What are the steps to make it happen?
  • Aged care worker registration delayed until next year
  • Dutton readies for fight to keep $2.9b fuel excise cut going
  • Thorpe’s ‘coloniser’ Queen oath out of order as former leaders find backbench
  • Farmers urge tax break for pensioners to unleash grey army of fruit pickers
  • Best of cartoons, August 1, 2022
  • ‘The government is refusing to listen’: Rolling action to impact NSW trains
  • ‘Completely unfair’: Family takes WestConnex acquisition to the High Court
  • Race to save endangered orchids has some wins, some wombats
  • Any change to Constitution merits consulting populace

  • Crashed out on the Ottoman
  • Wounded pride: Is Sydney ready to host the biggest LGBTQ+ event?

  • ‘Timid and upset’: Mystery deepens over Saudi sisters’ deaths
  • For the sake of NSW, Stuart Ayres must resign
  • Stuart MacGill had money problems, court told, as alleged kidnappers seek bail
  • Brawl breaks out outside Leichhardt Oval after Sydney FC soccer match
  • Guy Sebastian’s ex-manager awaits decision on third attempt to detain him
  • ‘Last year, the FBI foiled a kidnap plot. Now a killer is dispatched to deal with me’
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • ‘Justice has been delivered’: Al-Qaeda leader Ayman al-Zawahiri killed in US airstrike

  • Russia using Ukrainian power plant as ‘nuclear shield’: US
  • ‘We can just kill you’: Myanmar junta deploys torture, death threats in reign of terror
  • Fears ex-Putin adviser’s illness is ‘poisoning’
  • ‘Non-threatening’: US warns China not to use Pelosi’s expected Taiwan trip to escalate tensions
  • Eagle eyes are on Taiwan, but is China distracting us from a ‘second Pearl Harbour’?
  • ‘Very serious interest’: Indonesia wants AUKUS submarines monitored by UN watchdog
  • School students help overturn 329 year old conviction for last Salem ‘witch’
  • Ukrainian grain ship departs Odesa for the first time since Russian invasion
  • WATCH LIVE: Billionaire, premier to learn their fates in defamation verdict
  • Qld koala boost after research cash swap
  • Royal Children’s Hospital waiting times blow out to eight hours
  • Hippos in tanks and a pig on the freeway: RSPCA WA celebrates 130 years of helping animals
  • Windy weather and damaging gusts to lash Victoria
  • Thomas family call last drinks at The Oaks with $175m price tag
  • ASX falls ahead of Reserve Bank interest rate decision
  • The RBA weapon that will help push rates up further
  • CSL clears hurdles to settle $16.4b Vifor deal
  • Facebook must ditch Zuckerberg if it is to prosper
  • Elon Musk has made his mark but I am not proud of him, says his father
  • Meet the Russian architect of Putin’s economic counter-attack
  • Political throat-clearing no substitute for a real productivity agenda
  • Gas giants hold emergency talks as minister threatens intervention
  • Sydney start-up Regrow Ag first to harness Google Earth Engine
  • Global malt supplier shrugs off share price slide
  • Gloves are off: Government versus the gas giants
  • The Wrap: ASX edges higher to kick off August in style
  • NAB chairman Phil Chronican points to risks of financing new gas projects
  • Afterpay warns of spike in scam texts ‘across the financial services industry’
  • Billionaire Scott Farquhar’s bid for renewable energy firm rejected
  • Thai cave rescue drama gripping but key Australian element left out
  • ‘Glorified function centre’: Powerhouse plans condemned in public submissions
  • How a rowdy pub became an unexpected haven for Melbourne poets
  • Thirty years on, A League of Their Own finally comes out of the closet
  • From Stranger Things to stranger danger: Why this director is rejecting ’80s nostalgia
  • The top 10 books you should read in August
  • Meet you under the colour-field: bold art coming to a station near you
  • Why is everyone mad at celebrities flying private jets?
  • Yes, Hunted is enjoyably stupid TV ... and I’ve watched every minute
  • Workplace training with a celebrity twist
  • Climate, inflation hang over Diggers and Dealers forum
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Washington Post: 2 Aug, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • U.S. agency warns of Afghan famine, more suppression of women’s rights
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: First grain shipment leaves Ukraine as Zelesnky urges security for future food exports

  • High-ranking U.S. officials don’t usually go to Taiwan. Here’s why.

  • Tensions between Kosovo and Serbia: Why NATO is ‘prepared to intervene’
  • Kosovo-Serbia tensions flare; NATO peacekeepers track border protests

  • Panama teachers end long strike that set off wider protests

  • 2 die in McKinney Fire, now California’s largest wildfire this year

  • An 18-year-old lifeguard helped deliver a baby on a YMCA pool deck
  • A reporter went to cover an execution. She was told her skirt was too short.
  • Man with assault rifle arrested near Iranian American writer’s Brooklyn home
  • Post Politics Now: Biden says ‘justice has been delivered’ with killing of al-Qaeda’s Zawahiri
  • First Jan. 6 defendant convicted at trial receives longest sentence of 7 years
  • Biden covid case highlights confusing CDC guidance on ending isolation
  • W.Va. politician who apologized for Jan. 6 now writing defiant book
  • Visa could be liable in suit over child sexual abuse material on Pornhub, other sites
  • Rents and home prices are still soaring, but at a slower pace
  • Banana Boat sunscreen for hair and scalp is recalled over carcinogen
  • Government workers feel inflation’s pinch as wages lag
  • Twitter is probing Elon Musk’s social circle in broad legal requests
  • The latest video game controller isn’t plastic. It’s your face.
  • Cell carrier privacy settings to change now
  • A road map to homeownership for consumers with thin credit files
  • What to do if sellers don’t give you all the house keys at closing
  • Two-bedroom, two-bathroom condo in Bowie, Md., lists for $215,000
  • Food Prices Are Falling. Why Is There Still a Hunger Crisis?
  • HSBC’s Promises Won’t Satisfy Ping An for Long
  • Bangladesh Outshone India. Now It Must Learn from its Neighbor

  • Understanding Monkeypox and How Outbreaks Spread
  • How the 15% US Minimum Corporate Tax Would Work
  • What’s Driving the US-China Spat Over Audits and Delisting
  • What the Hong Kong Dollar Peg Is and Why It Matters
  • Farewell to two members of Black America’s greatest generation
  • Don’t sink this bill, Sen. Sinema
  • Zawahiri was in ‘downtown Kabul’ because of Biden’s disastrous Afghanistan withdrawal
  • Ignore GOP scaremongering about Dems’ tax plans. They’re worth doing.
  • What might help Democrats’ prospects? A focus on workers’ dignity.
  • The latest Trump grift? Burying Ivana at their golf club.
  • Bill Russell was a basketball giant who cared about all his teammates
  • The Inflation Reduction Act is anything but
  • Kyrsten Sinema has all the power right now. She should use it.
  • Biden has tested positive again. That’s no reason to avoid Paxlovid.
  • Did Russia or Ukraine slaughter Ukrainian POWs? It’s not a close call.
  • ‘Nope’ director Jordan Peele mocks his audience. They love him for it.
  • Mexico’s energy policy gives the U.S. no choice but to push back
  • We might finally get the truth about allegations against Justin Fairfax
  • A beloved butterfly is in peril. It’s not alone.
  • 3 Senate races show how MAGA candidates are dragging down the GOP
  • ‘Fox & Friends,’ at its transparent best
  • The ticking time bomb still threatening the big climate deal
  • The Electoral Count Act must be fixed. A new proposal doesn’t go far enough.
  • Wisconsin has a chance to stand up for victims of sex trafficking. It must.
  • The big guy’s little thugs
  • Where no one had gone before
  • Cartoon by Jimmy Margulies
  • Why designating Russia a state sponsor of terrorism is a bad idea
  • The Virginia GOP is eroding Democrats’ hold on Latino voters
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Korea Herald: 2 Aug, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Asan Medical Center nurse dies after collapsing while on duty
  • Ruling party to hold nat'l committee to decide on transition to emergency leadership
  • Presidential office to look into allegation of Buddhist figure if necessary
  • Electricity use hits all-time high in July amid heat wave
  • Police bureau officially comes into being
  • S. Korean defense chief to visit Australia for talks on arms industry cooperation
  • [Graphic News] N. Korean economy shrinks for 2nd year in 2021 on sanctions, pandemic: BOK
  • New infections at over 3-month high; imported cases at all-time high
  • N. Korea reports no new suspected COVID-19 cases for 4th day: state media
  • Blinken reaffirms US commitment to NPT, citing threats posed by N. Korea, Iran
  • [Hwang’s China and the World] India is not the weakest link in the Quad
  • Ruling party leadership posed to get overhaul, making Lee Jun-seok’s return unlikely
  • Backlash against lowering elementary school entry age intensifies
  • What we need is consistency: Unmasking the criminal suspects
  • S. Korea’s Transport Minister mentions Uber as last resort to solve late-night taxi shortage
  • BTS can prepare for overseas concerts while serving in military, says Defense Minister
  • [Newsmaker] Negligence outstrips alcohol as leading cause of road deaths
  • Power demand hits record high amid heat wave
  • Revision seeks to allow battery subscription for EVs
  • Samsung publishes paper on artificial muscle actuators for VR devices
  • Hyundai Motor unveils smart city vision in Singapore
  • LG Chem looks to begin final stage of clinical trials for gout drug with FDA
  • SK Bioscience eyes Europe as market for COVID-19 vaccine
  • SK hynix to mass-produce CXL memory chips from 2023
  • SK Geo Centric, Tokuyama to build plant for wafer washing agent
  • Starbucks tops most preferred coffee chain in S. Korea: survey
  • Seoul shares open lower amid US-China tension, recession woes
  • S. Korea's inflation at near 24-year high in July on high energy, food prices
  • BOK signals rate hike slowdown while slashing growth forecast
  • Seoul shares up for 6th day on speculation over less-aggressive US rate hikes
  • S. Korea’s accumulative trade deficit hits 66-year high
  • Photography evolves, diversifies by taking different formats
  • BTS to resume 'Run BTS' variety show after 10-month break
  • BTS could be allowed to perform overseas during military service: defense minister
  • [Herald Interview] Lee Byung-hun channels fear of flying for new role
  • [Herald Interview] Song Kang-ho wishes audience to find hope through ‘Emergency Declaration’
  • Trendy rapper Zico returns with 4th EP ‘Grown Ass Kid’
  • [Today’s K-pop] Seventeen’s repackage album enters Billboard 200 at No. 4
  • [Andrew Sheng] Is the falling yen a cause for concern?
  • [Editorial] Shrinking population
Korea Herald News articles

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 2 Aug, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 2 Aug, 2022 published at 2022-08-02 04:03AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Coronavirus: recovered Hongkongers should still get three shots of Covid-19 vaccine, says expert
  • US targets Hong Kong and UAE firms it says ship Iranian oil to East Asia
  • Hong Kong national security police arrest immigration officer, government technician over online messages
  • Hong Kong education authorities reject survival plans by 2 under-enrolled secondary schools
  • Car dealers propose easing warranty restrictions on maintenance, repair services after probe by Hong Kong’s competition watchdog
  • Hong Kong police arrest 60 suspects in 44 deception cases involving HK$75 million in two-week operation
  • Mirror concert mishap: the unanswered questions haunting Hong Kong’s biggest boy band
  • Hong Kong Observatory says July was city’s hottest month on record
  • Hong Kong must have a plan to end quarantine if it is to keep international financial hub status, retain talent, fund managers say
  • Hong Kong’s graft-buster charges senior fire services officer with fraud for allegedly swindling bank, corporation out of HK$7.8 million
  • Hong Kong slips into recession as economy shrinks by 1.4 per cent in second quarter
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong health experts recommend Covid jabs for children as young as 6 months
  • People’s Liberation Army’s Hong Kong garrison holds first flag-raising ceremony at Central designated zone marking military anniversary
  • Mirror concert accident: authorities clamp down on mid-air installations at venues after Hong Kong dancer suffers serious injuries
  • Why so much talk about Nancy Pelosi’s visit to Taiwan, and what does it mean for US-China relations?
  • ‘Confident superpower or insecure?’ Beijing’s reaction if Pelosi goes to Taiwan may be telling
  • China’s state-run semiconductor fund engulfed in scandal as Beijing probes three senior executives for alleged corruption
  • China-backed Nairobi Expressway opens in Kenya, paving way for more cautious financing model
  • China can afford to abandon its GDP target, but not its promise of economic growth
  • Massive RCEP trade deal fails in areas where smaller bilateral pacts in Asia-Pacific shine
  • British MPs plan visit to Taiwan as tension with China simmers: report
  • China may grant Brazilian soymeal export permits in two months
  • Nancy Pelosi will visit Taiwan, reports say, as China vows ‘strong measures’ in response
  • Pelosi, Taiwan and climate: why military conflict might be good for the planet
  • Tech war: China’s semiconductor ambitions face reality check amid reports US is set to widen ban on exports of equipment to country
  • Pelosi’s likely Taiwan plans cast shadow over PLA’s 95th year celebrations
  • Taiwan may try to keep high-level Pelosi visit ‘low-profile’, analysts say
  • Zambia cancels US$1.6 billion Chinese loans and halts infrastructure projects in move to avoid debt crisis
  • China releases plan to guide carbon-intensive industries to reach peak emissions by 2030
  • China’s Communist Party mouthpiece launches NFT research project to ‘guide’ digital art development
  • ‘Lightning carrier concept’: lessons for China in US Navy’s use of amphibious ships as small carriers
  • US envoy to Japan seeks to counter China’s ‘economic coercion’ with cooperation on chips, batteries and energy
  • North Korean hackers are posing as crypto freelancers to secure remote work abroad, researchers say
  • Singapore hawkers feel the sting of rising prices, ‘no thanks to Russia’
  • Philippines’ Fidel Ramos sought Beijing ties but also showed South China Sea defiance
  • Elizabeth Zhong murder: Chinese millionaire Fang Sun jailed for more than 18 years
  • Shinzo Abe assassination: Japan ruling party says parliamentary eulogy for ex-PM postponed
  • India investigates Asia’s first suspected moneypox-linked death
  • Singapore says stable US-China ties crucial as Nancy Pelosi keeps mum over Taiwan stop
  • Asia’s richest men prepare to battle in India as Gautam Adani enters Mukesh Ambani’s turf
  • US military wants to expand ties with New Zealand, says Pacific commander who called China ‘destabilising’
  • Conjoined twins separated in 27-hour surgery with help of virtual reality
  • Did US use secret ‘ninja’ missile to kill al-Qaeda leader Zawahiri?
  • Big tech should share Europe network costs, say France, Italy, Spain, but some fear threat to net neutrality
  • US to send US$550 million of weapons and ammunition to Ukraine forces, official says
  • US kills al-Qaeda chief Ayman al-Zawahiri in Kabul drone strike
  • US Senate likely to delay bill on gay marriage until September
  • 80 men appear in South Africa court for gang rape of 8 women during music video shoot
  • UN chief Antonio Guterres warns world is one step away from ‘nuclear annihilation’
  • Vatican says Pope Francis to visit Kazakhstan; expected to meet Russian Orthodox patriarch who justified Ukraine invasion
  • UK PM race: Foreign Secretary Liz Truss wins cabinet backing as voting begins to elect new Conservative leader
  • Iran nuclear chief: we have technical means to produce atomic bomb, but have no intention of doing so
  • ‘Relief for world’ as first Ukraine grain ship since start of war departs Odesa
  • ‘Living with Covid-19’: where the pandemic could go next
  • Ukraine-Russia war hangs over UN meeting on nuclear weapons
  • Truss vs Sunak: Divisive Margaret Thatcher looms over UK leadership race
  • What buyers of uncompleted properties overseas from Hong Kong should do to protect their interests
  • Alibaba, HSBC, AIA lead losses in all Hang Seng members on Taiwan tensions as China promises ‘firm and strong’ measures against Pelosi’s visit
  • Australia Foreign Investment Review Board fee hike is likely to weigh on home prices, property investment, analysts say
  • Chinese EV maker Nio planning mass market foray with new brand, to challenge Toyota and Volkswagen with compact cars priced below 200,000 yuan
  • Hong Kong can tap IPO potential of tech start-ups with innovative listing reforms
  • China’s top EV trio suffer sales setback in July as heatwave deters consumers but new models inspire second-half confidence
  • CSRC, China’s securities watchdog, pledges to prevent ‘abnormal fluctuations’ in embattled stock market
  • Chinese studio Bona Film, maker of Battle at Lake Changjin, set for Shenzhen IPO after Nasdaq departure
  • China’s new home prices in July record first decline in eight months as mortgage-boycott crisis weighs on sentiment
  • Carry traders are using the weakening euro to score major wins in the emerging markets
  • TikTok’s e-commerce push heats up in Southeast Asia with new shopping campaigns as retail initiatives in the West hit a snag
  • K-pop star Kang Daniel on his SpongeBob SquarePants collaboration, upcoming solo concert debut and his music company Konnect
  • Chiang Kai-shek used Western journalists to promote his Nationalist cause abroad – including a controversial Australian radio host
  • The Sandman, Netflix comic-book fantasy series, is out on August 5 – everything you need to know about the Neil Gaiman DC Comics adaptation
  • Multiple wedding dresses for Meghan, Jennifer Lopez, Britney Spears – more people are saying yes to two or more outfits for their big day
  • Affordable Art Fair Hong Kong 2022 focuses on young artists from the city and local art galleries, many of them recently opened
  • What’s Buckingham Palace like? Inside the British royals’ State Rooms, as tours open for the first time since 2019
  • K-drama midseason recap: Extraordinary Attorney Woo – Netflix drama series about a lawyer with autism will give you all the feels
  • Urban farming a form of mental health therapy for rooftop gardeners in Hong Kong, and it fosters a sense of community and healthy eating, advocates say
  • Why is Hermès skipping out on the second-hand luxury market? Like LVMH, the Birkin bag maker is disinterested in sustainable, pre-owned fashion platforms
  • Meet Kang Tae-oh, breakout star in Extraordinary Attorney Woo: he made his K-pop debut with 5urprise before landing K-drama Run On with SNSD’s Sooyung – and went to the same uni as BTS’ Jin
  • Ariana Grande’s 20 most iconic fashion looks ever: inside her style transformation from Nickelodeon teen starlet to pop princess donning Versace, Alexander McQueen and Giambattista Valli
  • Where is Kelis now? She’s bravely beefing with Beyoncé and Pharrell for sampling her song on ‘Renaissance’, but the Milkshake singer-turned-chef has been quietly grieving her husband’s death too
  • Who was Natalie Wood, Elvis Presley’s actress ex-girlfriend? She dated the King of Rock ‘n’ Roll and Frank Sinatra before marrying Robert Wagner twice – but why is her death still controversial?
  • Yes, the Aurora Diamante fountain pen costs US$1.47 million – here’s why, plus 3 more super-fancy writing pieces from Cartier, Montegrappa and Mont Blanc
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 2 Aug, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Push to build local skate park a lesson for youth, city alike
  • Russian’s violin that survived the A-bomb touches Ukrainians’ souls
  • Olympic exec allegedly took a cut from Aoki’s sponsorship fees
  • Kishida hints he may reclassify COVID-19 into a lower category
  • Actress’s love of sumo pays off big time with post on yokozuna council
  • Replica of famed Shinden wartime fighter finally graces museum
  • Kishida calls on LDP lawmakers to explain ties to religious group
  • Kishida pushed against his inner circle to attend NPT meeting
  • Japanese man in his 20s detained in Myanmar as he filmed protest
  • Boeing to establish R&D facility on SAF, electrification in Japan
  • ANA, JAL stay hopeful on year helped by strong demand
  • Insurance firms unveil heatstroke policies amid hotter summers
  • Survey: Prices of over 6,300 food items expected to rise in October
  • Companies migrating to Hokkaido amid the pandemic
  • White House decries China rhetoric over Pelosi Taiwan visit
  • Shenzhen accelerates China’s driverless car dreams
  • China air force, referring to Taiwan, says it can safeguard ‘territorial integrity’
  • No reported damage in Philippines from Chinese rocket debris
  • Ramos, ex-Philippine leader who helped oust dictator, dies
  • U.N. chief warns world is one step from ‘nuclear annihilation’
  • 1st ship carrying Ukrainian grain leaves the port of Odesa
  • Pelosi lands in Singapore to kick off Asia tour
  • JR Tokai runs its first hybrid train for tourists to Takayama
  • EDITORIAL: NPT must survive crisis to pursue goal of nuclear disarmament
  • VOX POPULI: Politicians’ ties with Unification Church need to be scrutinized
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: Guardian: 2 Aug, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Chinese police to give ‘management and leadership’ training to Solomon Islands officers
  • Ayman al-Zawahiri: al-Qaida leader killed in US drone strike in Afghanistan, Joe Biden says
  • World ‘one miscalculation away from nuclear annihilation’, UN chief says
  • California declares state of emergency over monkeypox
  • Large tortoise on the track stops Norwich to Stansted trains
  • Polio found in New York wastewater month before confirmed case emerged
  • Russia-Ukraine war latest: what we know on day 160 of the invasion
  • Blistering heatwave suspected in 14 Oregon deaths
  • New Zealand MPs warned not to use TikTok over fears China could access data
  • Premier League 2022-23 preview No 13: Manchester City
  • US says Beijing has no reason to turn Pelosi’s expected Taiwan visit into a ‘crisis’
  • Sexual assault case against director Paul Haggis dropped in Italy
  • ‘Hard to enjoy’: Kyle Chalmers wins gold as Glaetzer defies late handlebar change
  • Gueye poised to rejoin Everton from PSG as Aaron Ramsey signs for Nice
  • Climate endgame: risk of human extinction ‘dangerously underexplored’
  • Truss v Sunak: how do Tory PM contenders differ on policy?
  • Shia factions mass in Baghdad raising fears of more unrest
  • Ryder Cup captain Luke Donald still in dark over Europe’s LIV players
  • Sole parents like myself are facing homelessness. It’s time our PM realised this is not the Australia he grew up in | Anonymous
  • BeReal: the Instagram rival app wants me to be ‘authentic’. I can’t imagine anything more boring
  • Food prices soaring in developing world amid Ukraine crisis, World Bank finds
  • Gazprom daily gas output in July lowest since 2008, analysis suggests
  • Marion Rousse hails success of ‘proper women’s Tour de France’ | Jeremy Whittle
  • African nations expected to make case for big rise in fossil fuel output
  • Bill Russell was the NBA’s king of championship rings – and far more important matters
  • The Lionesses were astounding in a tournament that had the wow factor | Karen Carney
  • First Europe, next the world: Lionesses set their sights on global domination
  • Who do the Tory leadership candidates think they are impressing with their spiteful posturing on trans issues? | Zoe Williams
  • Browns’ Watson suspended six games over alleged serial sexual misconduct
  • Rapper Mystikal arrested on rape charges
  • I was England captain when women’s football struggled for attention: how far we’ve come | Faye White
  • Beyoncé to change Renaissance lyric containing ableist slur
  • Germany puts coal power plant back on network after gas supply cut
  • Arms dealer ‘100% sure’ Russian agents behind blast at Bulgarian depot
  • ‘Assassin with loaded AK47’ faces federal charges for surveilling home of Iranian-American journalist
  • Nichelle Nichols was my hero and a groundbreaking figure for Black women | Mikki Kendall
  • ‘The power we had was astonishing’: ex-soldiers on Israel’s government in the occupied territories
  • ‘Like an alien obelisk’: space debris found in Snowy Mountains paddock believed to be from SpaceX mission
  • Womad festival – Gilberto Gil helps celebrate 40 years of blazing trails
  • Oh my days! Midnight comes a fraction sooner as Earth spins faster
  • Female journalist told skirt too short when reporting on Alabama execution
  • ‘People love to feel part of the process’: how the TikTok teaser turbo-charged the song of the summer race
  • ‘We sent Kate Bush a thank you letter’: how Utah Saints made Something Good
  • Joy, relief and celebration confusion: the Lionesses win Euro 2022 – Football Weekly
  • Break stuff! How Limp Bizkit, rioting fans and a huge candle handout led to a music festival fiasco
  • Hotter than Dubai: US cities at risk of Middle Eastern temperatures by 2100
  • UK farmers count cost as heatwave kills fruit and vegetable crops
  • ‘I hate them’: Dmitry Medvedev’s journey from liberal to anti-western hawk
  • Nichelle Nichols obituary
  • ‘Reality is scary’: climate culture war heats up for UK meteorologists
  • Revelations since Shinzo Abe death shed light on Moonies’ influence
  • Rachel Roddy’s recipe for crab linguine with lemon and herbs
  • Now come and watch us with our clubs, England’s Toone urges new fans
  • ‘At best it is brain-rotting’: Love Island will be banned in 50 years
  • Controversy, but louder: Stenson’s LIV Golf title takes backseat in Trumpland
  • Kelly’s winner sparks tears of joy everywhere and a wild sense of liberation | Sid Lowe
  • Single servings at low prices: how Unilever’s sachets became an environmental scourge
  • The climate crisis is so boring – but I also hate the idea of burning to death | Sofie Hagen
  • Only a country as complacent as the UK could give up its border privilege so easily | Nesrine Malik
  • Taliban policies risk de facto university ban for Afghan women, say officials
